destinations.evidence: Settlement fee calculation selects LLM metric pricing or HTTP byte pricing by precompile address and derives commitment/inclusion fees from input/output lengths rather than fixed 0.01 ETH constants.
LLM executor fees are computed from decoded response metrics and registry-backed model parameters, with errors for missing or undecodable output except explicit executor error responses.

equivalence_argument: The fixed three-fee settlement model is replaced by ritual-async-fees: HTTP uses input/output byte formulas, LLM uses decoded model/token metrics, and validator fee components scale with data size.
notes: Mapped in the port shared async-fees crate used by payload and block-verification paths.
rationale: The commit removes the three fixed settlement fee constants, adds explicit byte-based HTTP fee functions, adds LLM response decoding and gas computation, selects the LLM pricing path for `LLM_CALL_PRECOMPILE`, and computes commitment/inclusion fees from input and output lengths. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Async settlement transactions must compute executor, commitment, and inclusion fees from the async precompile input/output instead of using fixed 0.01 ETH fees: HTTP executor fee is base plus per-input and per-output byte fees, validator fees are byte-count gas estimates, and LLM executor fee is computed from decoded LLM response metrics when available with documented fallback behavior.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build settlement transactions for HTTP async metadata with two different input/output lengths and assert the executor, commitment, and inclusion fee fields change according to the byte formulas. Build an LLM settlement with ABI-encoded response metrics and assert the executor fee follows the decoded token/model metrics instead of the HTTP byte formula; then provide malformed or missing LLM output and assert the documented fallback path is used.
summary: Async settlement fees are dynamically calculated from precompile input/output and LLM response metrics.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Build HTTP settlements with different input/output lengths and LLM settlements with decoded metrics and assert fee fields vary according to the selected formulas.
title: Async settlement fees are dynamically priced
